plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) to zero.
Increase allocation to income ETF (QQQI) for stability.
Reduce concentration in SPY to meet mandate.
Add to strong performer CRWD and top scanner pick NVDA.
Hold a significant cash buffer for capital preservation.
Maintain tight stops across all positions.
conservative.read: This plan prioritizes capital preservation with a target of around 1.5% monthly. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration, aiming for lower drawdowns but also capping upside potential.

plan: Trim leveraged ETFs (SOXL) to a small allocation and exit others (SPAL).
Reduce concentration in SPY and trim QQQ to meet mandate.
Add new positions in top scanner setups NVDA and DDOG.
Maintain a moderate cash buffer for flexibility.
Balance broad index exposure with quality tech and limited leverage.
balanced.read: This balanced approach targets approximately 2.5% monthly growth while prioritizing risk management. It reduces concentration and leverage, blending index funds with quality tech and new high-potential setups.

plan: Increase allocation to leveraged ETFs SOXL and SPAL.
Reduce concentration in SPY and trim QQQ to allow for higher conviction adds.
Add significantly to strong performer CRWD and top scanner pick NVDA.
Maintain a smaller cash buffer to maximize invested capital.
Concentrate capital in highest-scoring, high-leverage setups.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan aims for ~4% monthly growth by leaning into leverage and high-conviction setups. It accepts significantly higher potential drawdowns and the risk of leveraged ETF decay, requiring strict stop discipline.

plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs and high-beta single names.
Exit volatile sector exposure (SMH).
Significantly increase allocation to income-focused QQQI.
Increase allocation to SPY for broad market exposure and potential distributions.
Hold a substantial cash buffer for stability.
Prioritize steady monthly distributions over growth.
income.read: This income-focused plan targets around 1% monthly distributions with low drawdowns. It shifts capital from growth-oriented assets to income ETFs and broad market index funds, prioritizing capital preservation and reliable income streams.
